ogTitle: Blanche Harbor vs Bletchley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Blanche Harbor and Bletchley.
heading: Blanche Harbor vs Bletchley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Blanche Harbor, South Australia and Bletchley, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bletchley has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Blanche Harbor (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Blanche Harbor or Bletchley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Blanche Harbor has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Bletchley scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Blanche Harbor scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Bletchley scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Blanche Harbor or Bletchley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bletchley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.77% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
